outbound_official.notes: The official MOFA page (mofa.gov.kn/travel/) lists all destinations under a single 'Visa-Free Countries' heading without breaking them into visa-on-arrival, e-visa, or ETA sub-categories. The page does not carry a last-updated date. Stay durations vary by destination (e.g., UK 6 months, Colombia 180 days, Peru 183 days, Fiji 120 days; most others 30–90 days). Mexico appears on the visa-required page with a note 'can travel on valid US visa', suggesting it is not unconditionally visa-free for KNA nationals. Mexico was excluded from visa_free accordingly. The MOFA travel section also links to a separate visa-required list (mofa.gov.kn/visa-requirements-for-stkitts-and-nevis-nationals/) which enumerates countries requiring advance visas.

visa_free.nationality: Commonwealth of Nations member states (as listed on the UK High Commission page)
visa_free.notes: The St. Kitts & Nevis High Commission London lists specific Commonwealth and non-Commonwealth nations as visa-exempt under the Visa (Exemption) Order 1985. Named countries include Canada, Australia, New Zealand, South Africa, and all Caribbean Commonwealth states plus European and GCC countries enumerated on the page. NOTE: India is NOT on this list — the High Commission page does not include India in the visa-exempt schedule. India holds an eTA-eligible status based on SKN's 2018 unilateral waiver for ordinary passport holders. Specific stay duration determined on arrival by Immigration.

visa_free.nationality: Organisation of American States (OAS) member states (except Dominican Republic)
visa_free.notes: OAS members explicitly listed as visa-exempt: Argentina, Bolivia, Brazil, Chile, Colombia, Costa Rica, Ecuador, El Salvador, Guatemala, Honduras, Mexico, Nicaragua, Panama, Paraguay, Peru, United States, Uruguay, Venezuela. Dominican Republic is explicitly excluded and requires a visa. Source: St. Kitts & Nevis High Commission London.
Listed on the St. Kitts & Nevis High Commission London visa-free list under the Visa (Exemption) Order 1985.

visa_free.nationality: Puerto Rico and U.S. Virgin Islands (US nationals)
visa_free.notes: The St. Kitts & Nevis High Commission London page explicitly lists 'Puerto Rico and the U.S. Virgin Islands' as a separate visa-free entry distinct from the United States of America listed under OAS members. These are US territories; their residents hold US passports and are therefore US nationals who are visa-free via the OAS/US waiver. The separate listing appears to be a clarifying note on the face of the official exemption order rather than a distinct nationality waiver.

Saint Kitts and Nevis allows Nigerian ordinary passport holders visa-free entry (SKN had granted this first; Nigeria reciprocated effective August 4, 2025). The September 2025 SKNIS article confirms the reciprocal nature: SKN had already allowed Nigerians visa-free entry. No maximum stay limit stated in the official SKNIS announcement. This entry covers the KN-side policy of admitting Nigerian ordinary passport holders visa-free.

Effective March 4, 2026, Saint Kitts and Nevis and Ghana signed a visa exemption agreement for holders of ordinary passports. This extends the pre-existing (2018) diplomatic/official/service passport waiver to all passport types. No maximum stay specified in the official announcement.

Listed on the St. Kitts & Nevis High Commission London visa-free list. Suriname is also a CARICOM member state (covered under the CARICOM bloc entry), so this entry is consistent with the CARICOM eTA-exempt grouping.

visa_free.nationality: CARICOM member nations (Antigua and Barbuda, Barbados, Belize, Dominica, Grenada, Guyana, Haiti, Jamaica, Montserrat, Saint Lucia, Saint Vincent and the Grenadines, Suriname, Trinidad and Tobago)
visa_free.notes: CARICOM member nation citizens are eTA-exempt and must complete the free Online Immigration and Customs ED Form instead. Confirmed as visa-free/eTA-exempt on official MOFA and SKNIS pages. Note: The Bahamas is a CARICOM member but not a CSME participant; may have different rules.

visa_free.nationality: OECS member states (Antigua and Barbuda, Dominica, Grenada, Montserrat, Saint Lucia, Saint Vincent and the Grenadines, Anguilla, British Virgin Islands)
visa_free.notes: OECS member state citizens are fully eTA-exempt per official MOFA and SKNIS pages. Must complete the free Online Immigration and Customs ED Form.

e_visa.nationality: All nationalities not otherwise exempt (visa-required countries)
e_visa.notes: Visitors requiring a visa may apply online through the official e-visa portal. Fee: US$100 for visitor visa; US$150 for student visa. Processing time approximately two weeks. Visa is emailed upon approval. Dominican Republic nationals explicitly require a visa. Stays under 24 hours from countries normally requiring visas do not require a visa. Official e-visa portal: evisa.stkittsnevisonline.com (note: evisa.gov.kn redirects to this address). The Embassy DC FAQ also references www.evisa.gov.kn as the entry point.

eta.nationality: All foreign visitors (universal eTA requirement, except exempt categories)
eta.notes: Effective 26 May 2025, every foreign visitor to St Kitts and Nevis must obtain an approved Electronic Travel Authorisation (eTA) before departing their country of origin. The eTA is not a visa; it is a digital permission to travel, valid for 90 days or until passport expiry, whichever is sooner. Applications submitted up to 90 days before travel. Standard fee: US$17 (discounted rate US$8.50 through 1 September 2025). Apply via www.knatravelform.kn or the 'St Kitts and Nevis e-Border' mobile app (Android/iOS). Fast-Track Arrival via facial biometric corridor operational from 2 June 2025. Saint Kitts and Nevis is the first CARICOM nation to implement an eTA system.

eta.nationality: eTA-exempt: Citizens/residents of Saint Kitts and Nevis, OECS member states, CARICOM member nations
eta.notes: Citizens and residents of Saint Kitts and Nevis, citizens of OECS member states, and CARICOM member nations do NOT need an eTA, but must still complete the free Online Immigration and Customs ED Form via the e-Border system. Additional eTA-service-charge-exempt categories: accredited diplomats on official duty, children under 2 years old, in-transit passengers, on-duty flight/vessel crew, and active military or search-and-rescue personnel.

Indian ordinary passport holders require an eTA to enter Saint Kitts and Nevis. India is NOT on the official visa-exempt schedule at the St. Kitts & Nevis High Commission London. SKN unilaterally waived visas for all Indian passport types (including ordinary) effective from its 2018 bilateral agreement (signed 16 February 2018, in force 9 October 2018); however, the formal agreement recorded by India's MEA only covers Diplomatic & Official/Service Passports (up to 90 days). For ordinary passport holders, India's MEA classifies SKN as 'Visa on Arrival' — but effective 26 May 2025, the eTA system replaced the prior entry mechanism. Wikipedia's visa policy of SKN (citing Timatic/IATA) places India in the 3-month eTA category. Apply via www.knatravelform.kn before departure. Fee: US$17.

visa_policy.notes: Since 26 May 2025 St Kitts and Nevis operates a universal Electronic Travel Authorisation (eTA) system administered through the official e-Border Management System (www.knatravelform.kn). All foreign visitors require an approved eTA prior to travel regardless of nationality; eTA is exempted only for SKN citizens/residents, OECS citizens, and CARICOM citizens (who instead file the free ED Form) and special categories. The St. Kitts and Nevis High Commission London publishes a visa-free country list under the Visa (Exemption) Order 1985 covering Commonwealth nations and OAS members (except Dominican Republic) plus a set of named European and GCC countries. An official online e-visa application is available at evisa.stkittsnevisonline.com (accessible also via evisa.gov.kn redirect) for nationalities that require a full visa, at US$100. The maximum initial stay is one month per Embassy DC FAQ; the Immigration authority determines actual duration upon arrival. Cruise passengers disembarking for less than 24 hours do not need a visa. While the eTA is valid for 90 days, the actual permitted stay is set by Immigration at the port of entry. Applicants with visitor status cannot work, access public funds, study, or become self-employed. New additions since last record: Nigeria (ordinary passports, visa-free effective before September 2025 from SKN side) and Ghana (ordinary passports, signed March 4 2026). The High Commission London page also separately lists Puerto Rico and US Virgin Islands — these are US territories whose residents hold US passports and are covered under the US/OAS waiver.

conditional_access.conditions: Holders of diplomatic, official, and service passports are permitted entry without a visa into Saint Kitts and Nevis for a period not exceeding 90 days. Ordinary Kazakh passports are not included in this waiver. Agreement signed 26 January 2024 at the Permanent Mission of the Republic of Kazakhstan to the United Nations in New York.

Saint Kitts and Nevis and Ghana signed a reciprocal visa exemption agreement for holders of Diplomatic and Official/Service Passports in November 2018 (stage 1). As of March 4, 2026, a separate agreement extended visa-free access to ordinary Ghanaian passport holders as well (see visa_policy.visa_free for Ghana ordinary passport entry). This conditional_access entry documents the original 2018 diplomatic/official/service passport agreement, which remains technically in force as the foundational agreement.

cbi.program_name: St. Kitts and Nevis Citizenship by Investment Programme

options.notes: Sustainable Island State Contribution (SISC) - minimum US$250,000 for main applicant or family up to 4 persons. Contributions support national development priorities including renewable energy, healthcare and education. Source: https://ciu.gov.kn/cbi-options/

Public Benefit Option (PBO) - minimum US$250,000 per unit contribution to Approved Public Benefit Projects that foster economic growth and job creation (infrastructure including schools, housing, arts centers, resorts, airport expansion). Source: https://ciu.gov.kn/press-st-kitts-and-nevis-citizenship-by-investment-faqs/
Developer's Real Estate Investment - minimum US$325,000 in an Approved Development (condo/unit in approved development). Resaleable after 7 years. Source: https://ciu.gov.kn/cbi-options/
Private Real Estate Sale (Single-Family Home) - purchase an exclusive private single-family home for at least US$600,000. Source: https://ciu.gov.kn/cbi-options/

cbi.processing_time: 160-180 days from submission to approval in principle
cbi.notes: World's first and oldest Citizenship by Investment Programme, established in 1984, administered by the Citizenship by Investment Unit (CIU). Dual citizenship explicitly allowed. No mandatory residency requirement to maintain citizenship once granted. Official CIU FAQs state processing takes 160-180 days from submission to approval-in-principle; actual investment made after approval-in-principle is issued. Applications must be submitted through Authorised Agents only; cannot apply directly to CIU. Due diligence fees: US$10,000 (main applicant), US$7,500 per dependent aged 16+. Post-approval fees: US$10,000-US$25,000 depending on dependents. Banned nationalities: Afghanistan, Belarus, Iran, Iraq, North Korea, Russia. Citizenship can be passed to future generations. No personal income tax, wealth tax, or inheritance tax. CIU website updated December 2025 (Chairman's Update reference noted) but no changes to minimum investment amounts confirmed. Sources: https://ciu.gov.kn/cbi-options/, https://ciu.gov.kn/application-process/, https://ciu.gov.kn/eligibility-criteria/, https://ciu.gov.kn/press-st-kitts-and-nevis-citizenship-by-investment-faqs/

validation_notes: ADDED Nigeria (NGA) to visa_policy.visa_free: SKNIS article (September 2025) confirms Saint Kitts and Nevis had already granted Nigerian ordinary passport holders visa-free entry; Nigeria reciprocated effective August 4, 2025. Previously missing from the record.

ADDED Ghana (GHA) to visa_policy.visa_free: Signed March 4, 2026 — ordinary passport holders of Ghana are now visa-free. Ghana was previously only in conditional_access (diplomatic/official/service only). The ordinary passport entry has been added to visa_free; the 2018 diplomatic/official/service conditional_access entry is retained as the foundational agreement.

ADDED Puerto Rico and U.S. Virgin Islands entry to visa_policy.visa_free: The High Commission London official page explicitly lists 'Puerto Rico and the U.S. Virgin Islands' as a separate entry distinct from 'United States of America'. This was omitted from the prior record. Note clarifies these are US territories whose residents hold US passports.

ADDED Suriname (SUR) to visa_policy.visa_free as a named individual entry: The High Commission London page lists Suriname separately in the Commonwealth section in addition to it being covered under CARICOM. Previously only listed under the CARICOM bloc entry.

CORRECTED e-visa source URL note: Embassy DC FAQ now references www.evisa.gov.kn as the entry point; confirmed that evisa.gov.kn redirects to evisa.stkittsnevisonline.com (the original portal). Both URLs documented. The record previously only cited evisa.stkittsnevisonline.com.

UPDATED eTA source URL in eta[1]: Changed from mofa.gov.kn to the more specific SKNIS May 2025 announcement page which directly states the exempt categories. The MOFA page confirmed eTA exemptions but the SKNIS article is the more authoritative announcement source.

CBI amounts CONFIRMED unchanged: All four investment route minimums (SISC US$250,000; PBO US$250,000; Developer's RE US$325,000; Private RE US$600,000) confirmed against live ciu.gov.kn/cbi-options/ page as of June 2026. No increases.

CORRECTED India (IND) classification from visa_free to eta: India is NOT on the St. Kitts & Nevis High Commission London visa-exempt schedule (verified June 2026). The prior 'Commonwealth nations' note incorrectly named India. SKN's unilateral 2018 waiver for ordinary Indian passports (SKNIS 16 Feb 2018) is real but India's MEA classifies SKN as 'Visa on Arrival' for ordinary passports; since 26 May 2025 eTA is required for all non-exempt visitors. India added to eta array (90 days, source: sknis.gov.kn 2018 announcement + Wikipedia Visa policy of SKN citing Timatic). Airline-aggregator claim of 'visa-free' is incorrect for Indian ordinary passport holders.

ADDED outbound_official field (June 2026): Official MOFA page mofa.gov.kn/travel/ confirmed as the authoritative KNA outbound visa-free destination list for KNA ordinary passport holders. Page lists ~150+ destinations as visa-free under a single category. No VOA/eTA/e-visa sub-categories on the page. No last-updated date shown.
gaps: The official per-nationality visa-required vs visa-exempt inbound list is not fully enumerated on any single official page; the High Commission London page (stkittsnevishcuk.gov.kn) provides the most complete groupings (Commonwealth + OAS members minus Dominican Republic + named European/GCC countries) but may not be exhaustive.

Maximum stay days for visa-free nationalities not stated verbatim on official pages; Embassy DC FAQ only states 'initially one month' as maximum initial period; actual duration set by Immigration officer at port of entry.
No official residency-by-investment / golden-visa / investor-residence programme found on official domains.
No official skilled / talent / startup / digital-nomad / fast-track immigration scheme found on official domains.
The e-visa portal (evisa.stkittsnevisonline.com) did not return usable scraped content; specific list of visa-required nationalities could not be verified from the portal directly.

No official source found for any conditional entry rule based on holding a third-country credential (US visa, Schengen visa, UK visa, Canada visa, residence permit, Green Card, etc.). Such rules are not mentioned on any official KN government domain.
No official source found for OCI (Overseas Citizen of India) cardholder special entry provisions.

Nigeria agreement details (max stay, exact passport types covered) not fully specified in the SKNIS announcement. Non-official sources (Vanguard Nigeria, BusinessDay) indicate ordinary, official, and diplomatic passports with up to 90 days, but these could not be verified from an official KN government page. Entry recorded as visa-free with null max_stay_days pending official confirmation.
Ghana ordinary passport agreement (March 4, 2026) effective date not explicitly stated; signed March 4, 2026 but operational commencement date unconfirmed from official sources.

Wikipedia (Visa policy of Saint Kitts and Nevis) cites additional agreements (e.g. Haiti diplomatic/official passport holders exempt for up to 6 months; San Marino, Togo, Georgia, Marshall Islands agreements signed in 2019). These could not be cross-verified on official KN government domains and were not recorded.

SKNIS reported agreements with Palestine (Oct 2021), Gabon (Oct 2021), Burkina Faso (Oct 2021), Eswatini (Nov 2020), Rwanda (Apr 2018), Indonesia (Sep 2017), Montenegro (Nov 2018), Georgia (Jul 2019) that may cover ordinary passports and therefore belong in visa_free rather than conditional_access; these nationalities are not enumerated on the High Commission London visa-free list and their inbound ordinary-passport scope could not be confirmed from the current official HC London page. Not recorded to avoid misclassification. [India 2018 agreement now resolved: ordinary passport holders require eTA effective May 2025; classified under eta array.]

Puerto Rico and U.S. Virgin Islands listing on the HC London page is as US territories — residents hold US passports. The separate listing may simply be a clarifying note in the Visa (Exemption) Order 1985 rather than a distinct nationality waiver. Recorded with explanatory note.

Saint Kitts and Nevis has NOT signed the 2025 OECS-CARICOM Free Movement Pact (which came into effect 1 October 2025 for Barbados, Belize, St. Vincent and the Grenadines, and Dominica). SKN remains a CARICOM and OECS member but without the expanded free movement rights of that new pact.

outbound_official MOFA page (mofa.gov.kn/travel/) does not distinguish visa-on-arrival, e-visa, or ETA requirements for destination countries — all are listed under a single 'Visa-Free Countries' heading. Some listed destinations (e.g., Maldives, Seychelles, Rwanda) may technically grant visa-on-arrival rather than advance visa-free entry; the MOFA page does not make this distinction. Counts reflect page-stated categories only.
